title: Persist created projects in the project list (staging vs source read model)
description:
On Save (mh3.5), the new project is optimistically prepended to the project list, but it VANISHES ON RELOAD: writes go to the staging DB (data/export/), while the read path (/api/projects) loads the source working copy, so the new project isn't there on the next fetch. The user noticed and expects saved projects to stay in the list.

Decide the read/write model so created projects persist:
- Option A: have the app READ from the staging DB once it exists (shows source-clone + your exports in one view) — but staging is a one-time clone of source, so existing projects can go stale vs the live DB.
- Option B: enable LIVE writes by default (currently env-gated via TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE, not exposed in UI) so the normal list (reads source) reflects new projects. Needs UI exposure + confirmation; live writes already have backup + BEGIN IMMEDIATE lock-detect + additive contract.
- Option C: keep staging but add an explicit 'import staging into source/NINA' action + refresh.

RESOLUTION CHOSEN (2026-06-19): Option B, via o2c's live-write toggle. The reader re-snapshots source on every read (working_copy.py get_working_copy refresh=True), so a live write shows up on the next /api/projects fetch. Implement the staging-vs-live write-target toggle (UI choice + confirm) as part of o2c (Phase 4); close 2ij when that lands. See [[ts-assistant-codebase]] export.py/EXPORT_DB. Discovered during mh3.5 testing.

title: Exposure plan groups — named reusable bundles (qiz.1 Stage 3)
description: Stage 3 of 3 (the big one). New TS-Assistant concept absent from NINA's schema: a named 'exposure plan group' bundling several exposure templates + desired counts (e.g. 'LRGB Dark Nebula', 'SHO Bright Target'), applied to a target in one pick. Stored app-side (store/plan_templates.py -> data/plan_templates.json, equipment.py pattern); PlanGroup { id, name, items:[{exposure_template_id, desired}] }; CRUD GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/plan-templates. Frontend: group manager + 'Apply plan group' dropdown that expands into editable exposure plans. Writer/export unchanged (groups expand to plans before Save). No NINA schema change.
close_reason: Merged in PR #12 (exposure plan groups: app-side store + CRUD + manager panel + apply-in-builder).

title: Evaluate removing custom survey dropdown in favor of Aladin's built-in layers control
description: Aladin Lite v3 has a built-in layers/stack control for choosing the image survey, overlapping our custom topbar dropdown (fed by /api/surveys). Evaluate whether the built-in control is functionally equivalent; if so, remove our dropdown (App.tsx survey picker, possibly /api/surveys). Nuance: our NSNS narrowband HiPS (simg.de/nebulae3/dr0_1/*) and the CORS-pinned DSS URL are NOT in Aladin's default registry, so to keep them available in the built-in control we'd register them via aladin.newImageSurvey/createImageSurvey/addNewImageSurvey so they show in the layers list. Decision: remove dropdown + register NSNS into Aladin's control, or keep the dropdown. Files: frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/sky/AladinView.tsx, backend/app/api/surveys.py.
close_reason: Evaluated empirically: Aladin's built-in Stack control lists only a fixed CDS registry (DSS, 2MASS, PanSTARRS, SDSS9, VTSS-Ha, ...) and does NOT include NSNS; createImageSurvey() registers a survey object but does not inject it into that selectable list. Built-in control is NOT functionally equivalent to our curated dropdown (one-click NSNS + CORS-pinned DSS), and it is complementary (also hosts overlay styling + add-HiPS-by-URL). Decision: KEEP the dropdown. Reopen if we later invest in registering NSNS into the Stack UI.

title: Profile-scoped everything: make NINA profile a top-level selector
description:
Target Scheduler is multi-profile: each NINA profile has its own projects, targets, and exposure templates. TS Assistant should model this too, with PROFILE as a top-level concept the user selects, scoping everything beneath it.

Make these all local to a specific profile (not global):
- Rigs / equipment (currently app-side equipment.json is global) -> per profile.
- Projects (and their targets) -> filtered/created under the active profile.
- Exposure templates (qiz.1) -> listed/created within the active profile (exposuretemplate.profileId).
- Exposure plan groups (qiz.1 Stage 3, app-side) -> tagged with profileId, only shown for the active profile.

Implications:
- A top-level profile picker in the UI (read distinct profileId from the DB; let the user choose the active one). Replaces the current ad-hoc 'NINA profile' free-text field in the Project builder and the global equipment list.
- Backend reads/writes filter by profileId; app-side stores (equipment, plan groups) gain a profileId key.
- New projects/templates inherit the active profile automatically.

Relates to: qiz.1 (exposure templates carry profileId), o2c (editing must stay within a profile), and the equipment store. Discovered 2026-06-19 — user wants profile to be the top-level organizing axis, mirroring Target Scheduler.

title: Named-object overlay: circle + label well-known/large objects on the sky view
description: Add an Aladin overlay that draws circles (sized to the object's angular extent) around larger / well-known deep-sky objects (e.g. Messier/NGC/IC catalog highlights) and labels them with their common/catalog names, so the user can orient and find framing targets at a glance. Frontend-only (Aladin graphicOverlay + labels/markers, like the existing FOV/draft overlays). Source the object list from a bundled catalog or a HiPS/MOC/VizieR query; togglable like the FOV-boxes layer. Future enhancement to the sky view.
close_reason: Named-object overlay shipped: bundled curated catalog (41 Messier/NGC/IC highlights) → extent circles + labels, topbar 'Named objects' toggle, verified via verify-cia.mjs

title: Live/production operating mode (read+write the real Target Scheduler DB)
description:
PR1 of the o2c split (user decision 2026-06-20): add a launch-time operating mode where the app reads AND writes the user's real Target Scheduler DB directly, instead of reading a snapshot and writing a staging copy. Selected by the existing TS_ASSISTANT_ALLOW_LIVE_WRITE env var (no in-app switch). Routes the EXISTING additive flows (create project, create template, undo) at the live DB; no UPDATE/DELETE here (that's o2c). Resolves 2ij for live users (created rows persist on reload because read target == write target).

Single chokepoint: config.resolve_active_mode() cached at startup -> {name, source, read_path, write_target, integrity_default, backup_window_min, live_error}; both working_copy.get_working_copy and export._resolve_target consult it so read/write can never desync.

Live-DB safety must-fixes (from safety review): (1) WAL-visible reads (mode=ro on a WAL DB NINA holds open can miss committed frames) -> open rw-by-discipline or PASSIVE-checkpoint first; (2) WAL-consistent backups (same issue in consistent_copy); (3) -wal size+mtime in backup_signature + backup_window_min=0 in live so coalescing can't reuse a pre-NINA-change backup (data-loss-on-restore); (4) run integrity_check OUTSIDE the held write lock in live (don't block NINA mid-capture on 158MB); (5) keep BEGIN IMMEDIATE busy->409 fail-fast, clearer message; (6) gzip backups in live. live+no-source -> live_error, never silent staging fallback. /api/health reports mode; UI shows persistent PRODUCTION banner.

title: Create new exposure templates (qiz.1 Stage 2)
description: Stage 2 of 3. Let the user create new exposure templates (full columns: filtername, gain, offset, bin, readoutmode, twilightlevel, moonavoidance*, dither, minutesOffset, defaultexposure). Backend: ExposureTemplateSpec + writer.write_exposure_template (full-column INSERT + guid) + export.create_exposure_template through the existing backup+BEGIN IMMEDIATE+provenance+_assert_additive wrapper (one INSERT, additive & undoable) + POST /api/exposure-templates. Frontend: template editor create form; on save refresh list + auto-select.

title: P3 — Database write / export path
description: Create Project + panel Targets + ExposurePlans in the DB safely: timestamped backup, schema/FK/EF validation, transactional write. Highest risk.
close_reason: P3 complete: mh3.1 (schema round-trip) → mh3.2 (backup+transactional additive writer) → mh3.3 (validation) → mh3.4 (export API) → mh3.5 (frontend Save). Framing→save→import-into-NINA loop works; writes to safe staging, additive-only + backup + undo + progress gate.

title: Schema round-trip fidelity test (gating)
description: Write a Project+Targets+ExposurePlans set, diff against a NINA-written DB to prove fidelity incl. __EFMigrationsHistory expectations. Gates the writer.
close_reason: Round-trip fidelity test landed: canonical schema module (byte-identical to NINA), faithful writer, 7 passing tests proving schema + data + RA-hours + FK fidelity and NO __EFMigrationsHistory/sqlite_sequence

title: Backup + transactional writer
description: Auto timestamped backup before write; transactional insert of Project + panel Targets + ExposurePlans matching exact schema.
close_reason: Backup + transactional additive-only writer landed: export.py orchestrator (BEGIN IMMEDIATE, FK+additive+integrity assertions, COMMIT/ROLLBACK), time-coalesced backups (backup.py), in-DB provenance table + progress gate (provenance.py), app-side ops bookkeeping (ops.py), validation seam (validate.py), undo_operation. Live-write opt-in gated. 12 tests, full suite 27 green.

title: Write validation (schema/FK/EF integrity)
description: Validate against introspected schema, FK integrity, and EF concurrency/migration constraints before committing.
close_reason: Write validation deepened: validate_schema (pre-write, in-txn) checks all canonical tables present + every writer-populated column exists + no unsatisfiable NOT-NULL drift (tolerates additive ALTER drift), profile_id required. Moved validate before INSERTs for clean errors. WRITTEN_COLUMNS contract in writer.py. 7 unit + 1 integration test; suite 34 green.

title: Export API endpoint
description: POST endpoint to create a project from staged edits; returns backup path + summary.
close_reason: Export API landed: POST /api/export (ProjectSpec of pre-expanded targets -> export_project -> ExportResult w/ backup_path+ids+counts) + POST /api/export/{op}/undo; ValidationError->422, busy/progress->409, ExportError->400. Registered in main.py. Writes to safe staging (live stays env-gated, not exposed). 3 TestClient tests; suite 37 green.

title: Frontend: create project from mosaic + pick exposure plan
description: Turn a framed mosaic into project+targets, choose exposure plan/template, trigger export.
close_reason: Frontend Save flow shipped: ProjectBuilder now has NINA-profile field (datalist from existing projects) + exposure-plan editor (filter/exposure/desired); Save button enabled when valid -> expands each mosaic group via mosaicPanels into per-pane targets, POSTs ProjectSpec to /api/export, shows result/error. Writes to staging. 10-check Playwright E2E (verify-save.mjs). tsc/lint clean.

close_reason: All P1 tasks complete; read-only sky overview verified end-to-end against real DB.

title: Verify end-to-end with real sample database

title: Edit existing Draft projects with no captured subframes
description:
Allow editing/modifying existing Target Scheduler projects (and their targets/exposure-plans) from TS Assistant — but ONLY when the project is in Draft state AND has no progress (no acquiredimage rows and every exposureplan.acquired==0). This is the modify/update path beyond the current additive-only create flow (mh3.5).

The safety guard already exists: has_progress(conn, project_id) in app/db/provenance.py (built in mh3.2) was added precisely as the guardrail so we never touch started work — reuse it here. The export orchestrator (app/db/export.py) already provides backup + BEGIN IMMEDIATE + validation + provenance + integrity assertions to build on.

Scope sketch:
- Backend: an update path (UPDATE/DELETE within the same transactional+backup wrapper) restricted to rows of a Draft, no-progress project; refuse otherwise (raise the existing ProgressError pattern). Decide whether edits are tracked in ts_assistant_provenance or treated as user-owned.
- API: a PUT/PATCH endpoint (or extend /api/export) to update an existing project from staged edits.
- Frontend: load an existing project into the ProjectBuilder draft (prefill name/profile/targets/panes/exposure-plans), edit, then Save = update instead of create. Only offer Edit on rows where state==draft and no subframes.

Note: this relaxes the strict additive-only contract for these specific safe rows, so it needs its own careful design — keep backup + transactional + the progress gate, and clearly distinguish 'edit my draft' from 'never touch started/active work'.

title: Coverage-area mosaic: drag a desired FOV rectangle, auto-divide into rig-FOV panels
description: User-envisioned interface: search/select an object, drag a desired field-of-view rectangle over it, and the app auto-divides it into mosaic panels of the current rig's FOV such that the defined area is fully covered (with overlap). Aladin Lite's rectangular select mode is the likely primitive. Future; builds on uhz.3-5 mosaic framing.
close_reason: Coverage-area mosaic shipped: Cover area mode drags an AoI rectangle, un-projects its screen corners via pix2world, and coverageToGrid() auto-divides it into rig-FOV panes (center+size+rotation from the AoI). Frontend-only. Verified geomcheck.mjs 24/24 + verify-p2.mjs 12/12 over the LAN origin.

title: P4 — Exposure plans, templates & polish
description: Exposure template browser/editor, exposure plan assignment UI, rule weights, and UX polish.

title: Exposure template browser/picker (qiz.1 Stage 1)
description: Stage 1 of 3. Read the user's existing exposure templates from Target Scheduler and let exposure plans reference one by Id (dropdown) instead of auto-creating a bare template. Backend: load_exposure_templates + ExposureTemplate model + GET /api/exposure-templates; ExposurePlanSpec.exposure_template_id reference path in writer (skip _ensure_template when set). Frontend: fetchExposureTemplates + template <select> in plan rows. Additive-safe; unblocks qiz.2.
close_reason: Merged in PR #11 (Stage 1: exposure-template browser/picker). Picker is mandatory; profile hidden.

description: View/edit project rule weights used by the planning engine.

title: Polish: target search via resolver + UX cleanup
description: Search box using Aladin Simbad/NED resolver; general UX cleanup.
close_reason: Dropped: target search is covered by Aladin's built-in Simbad search bar (showSimbadPointerControl) + the named-objects overlay; a custom resolver box would duplicate it. UX cleanup to be filed as concrete beads when found.

title: Audit + remove dev-only one-time-migration/compat code that can't occur in real usage
description:
Sweep the codebase for 'legacy'/compat/migration code that was added to paper over a ONE-TIME DEVELOPMENT event (e.g. a rename or refactor done in an unreleased branch) and that can never happen for a real user. Such code is permanent cruft + carries its own tests for a scenario that won't recur.

Concrete example that prompted this (already reverted): the 'plan groups -> exposure plan templates' rename also renamed the app-side store file (data/plan_groups.json -> data/plan_templates.json). I added a self-cleaning _migrate_legacy() + 2 tests so the dev's existing definition survived — but the rename never shipped, so no real user ever had plan_groups.json. The right fix was a one-time on-disk file move (done), not permanent handler code.

What to look for:
- Functions/branches named *_legacy / _migrate* / 'compat' / 'fallback' / 'one-time' that guard against states only reachable mid-development.
- Reading two filenames/schemas because one was renamed pre-release.
- Defaults/shims for data shapes that only existed on a dev's machine.
- Tests that exist solely to cover such migration shims.

Deliverable: a list of such spots with a recommendation (delete vs keep), and remove the clearly-unnecessary ones. Keep genuine migrations that real released users could hit. Guideline: prefer a one-time manual/ops action over permanent code for unreleased-only transitions.

Discovered 2026-06-20 (user feedback: don't over-complicate; no custom handler code + tests for one-time dev-only filename changes).

title: Show acquisition completion metric on project/target list + per-filter in target popup
description:
Surface how complete each target's acquisition is, so the user can see progress at a glance.

LIST (ProjectList.tsx): show a per-target (and ideally per-project rollup) 'completion' summary metric: '(desired subs / accepted subs / pending subs)' where pending = desired - accepted. Compact, alongside the existing target name/coords.

TARGET POPUP (AladinView.tsx target-marker click popup): show the fuller breakdown FILTER BY FILTER — one line per exposure plan: filter name, desired / acquired / accepted (and pending). i.e. the per-plan detail behind the summary number.

Data already exists — mostly a frontend display task: the API ExposurePlan model already carries desired/acquired/accepted (db/models.py, db/reader.py load_projects_conn projects them; api.ts ExposurePlan has desired/acquired/accepted). So no schema/reader changes needed; aggregate per target = sum over its exposure_plans.

Decisions to make during implementation:
- 'pending' definition: desired - accepted (frames still needed to hit the goal) vs desired - acquired (frames not yet captured). Probably desired-accepted (accepted = passed grading) since that's the real 'done' count; consider showing acquired-but-not-accepted (rejected/pending grading) too.
- Whether to roll the summary up to the Project level (sum across its targets) in addition to per-target.
- Visual treatment (e.g. 'L 30/28/2' per filter, or a small progress bar).

Discovered 2026-06-19 (user request).

title: Upgrade NSNS surveys from DR0.1 to DR0.2
description: Switch NSNS to the newer DR0.2 release. Verified DR0.2 HiPS layers (all CORS '*', base https://simg.de/nebulae3/dr0_2/<layer>): hbr8 (Ha+continuum color), halpha8 (Ha), oiii8 ([OIII]), sii8 ([SII]), rgb8 (RGB continuum). DR0.2 ADDS [OIII] and [SII] vs DR0.1; tc8 (true color) and sho8 are 404 (absent in DR0.2). Action: update backend app/api/surveys.py NSNS entries to dr0_2 paths and add OIII/SII/RGB variants; decide whether to keep DR0.1 tc8 for true-color. Verify rendering headlessly. File: backend/app/api/surveys.py.
close_reason: Done: NSNS surveys now use DR0.2 (https://simg.de/nebulae3/dr0_2/<layer>): hbr8, halpha8, oiii8, sii8, rgb8 — adds [OIII], [SII], RGB continuum. True Color kept on DR0.1 (no tc8 in DR0.2). Verified headless: all DR0.2 layers load CORS-clean and [OIII] renders over NGC 7000.

Given center RA/Dec + panel FOV (rig) + NxM + overlap% + PA -> per-panel centers + corner polygons. NO backend astro/mosaic.py / astropy. Per-panel centerRa/centerDec is the hand-off the P3 writer (mh3.*) persists as Target rows.

Verified via geomcheck.mjs (11/11) + verify-p2.mjs (11/11).

New Project -> default target placed by Aladin search or click/drag; per-target panes, overlap (panes>1 only), rotation; target list add/select/remove. ProjectDraft -> one TS Project (isMosaic if any target panes>1); each target expands to per-pane TS Target rows at save (P3).

Verified via geomcheck.mjs (11/11) + verify-p2.mjs (11/11).

title: Equipment editor: Save does not persist changes (empty-id bug)
description: Repro: click + (creates 'New Rig'), rename it / change settings, click Save -> changes are NOT saved. Likely root cause: EquipmentPanel.add() calls createEquipment({id:'', ...BLANK}) which POSTs an EMPTY string id. Backend EquipmentInput uses the provided '' rather than minting one (pydantic default_factory only fires when the field is ABSENT), so profiles get stored with id=''. Save then does PUT /equipment/{id} with an empty id, which does not match the /equipment/{profile_id} route (404), and the rejected promise is unhandled so no error surfaces. Fixes: (a) frontend createEquipment should omit id; (b) backend POST should always generate a fresh id (ignore/!require client id) — make EquipmentInput.id Optional and mint on create; (c) add error handling in EquipmentPanel.save() so failures are visible. Files: frontend/src/panels/EquipmentPanel.tsx, frontend/src/api.ts, backend/app/api/equipment.py, backend/app/equipment.py.
close_reason: Fixed: POST /equipment now mints a fresh id (empty client id was being stored, breaking the subsequent PUT). Added error surfacing in EquipmentPanel save/add/delete. Verified headless: create -> rename -> Save -> reload -> persisted.
